extractions.content: Landscape Overview table categorizing the DF tool ecosystem into six distinct categories: (1) Legends Browsers (LegendsViewer-Next, LegendsBrowser, LegendsBrowser2) -- XML export files, batch parse in-memory web UI; (2) Live Game Servers (weblegends) -- DFHack C++ memory, real-time HTTP per-request render; (3) Autonomous Agents (df-ai) -- DFHack C++ memory, tick-based reactive loop; (4) Narrative Generators (df-narrator) -- XML export files, score + template LLM-sized output; (5) Infrastructure Tools (dfhack-client-python, DwarfFortressLogger, myDFHackScripts, DwarvenSurveyor, df-structures) -- mixed RPC/memory/Lua/XML; (6) Mod Management (DF-Modloader, ModHearth, PyLNP, PyDwarf, Nexus Mod Manager) -- filesystem + DFHack Lua.
extractions.reasoning: PRD Section 1.3 states Chronicler's unique position but does not include the structured landscape categorization table. This taxonomy of competing tools by category and access pattern is entirely absent from the Master.

extractions.content: Reference Repository Inventory table with per-repo detail: LegendsViewer-Next (C#/Vue.js, ~15,000 lines, key contribution: Leaflet map, Cytoscape family tree, Chart.js viz, 115+ event types, Vue detail pages); LegendsBrowser (Java, ~8,000 lines, SAX streaming XML, SVG family tree, D3 chord diagram, Bootstrap popovers); LegendsBrowser2 (Go, ~12,000 lines, 132 event types, custom XML tokenizer, context-aware rendering, autocomplete search); weblegends (C++, ~6,000 lines, 94 event handlers, 40+ death causes, perspective-aware linking, live DFHack memory); df-narrator (Python, ~2,000 lines, 4 scoring formulas, HF_FIELDS set, rival detection, Markdown world summary); df-ai (C++, ~25,000 lines, full fortress AI: population, military, stocks, construction, trade management); dfhack-client-python (Python, ~1,500 lines, RPC protocol implementation, binary frame format); DwarfFortressLogger (Java, ~3,000 lines, 29 memory sections, Dwarf Therapist-compatible layout system); myDFHackScripts (Lua, ~2,000 lines, death cause lookup, citizen detection, eventful hooks, polling patterns); DwarvenSurveyor (C#/Unity, ~1,500 lines, XML coordinate parsing, biome rendering, site taxonomy); df-structures (XML, ~50,000 lines, canonical memory layout, 144 event types, all entity fields); DF-Modloader (Python, ~1,700 lines, raw file compiler, EDIT/SELECT/CUT handling, object template system); ModHearth (C#, ~2,000 lines, DFHack mod memory query, info.txt parsing, conflict detection); PyLNP (Python, ~3,000 lines, three-way merge algorithm, baseline management, merge status tracking); PyDwarf (Python, ~2,500 lines, doubly-linked token model, round-trip raw editing); Nexus Mod Manager (C#, ~50,000 lines, plugin architecture, category browsing, multi-game mod management); Dwarf Therapist (C++, ~20,000 lines, memory layout system -- not directly usable via remote access).
extractions.reasoning: The Master PRD references '17+ Repository Research Reports' in its header but contains no actual repository inventory table with line counts, languages, or key contributions. This is entirely absent from the Master.

extractions.content: Feature Inventory Summary with size-categorized counts per component (S=days, M=1-2 weeks, L=2-4 weeks, XL=1+ months): World History Visualizer: S=7, M=8, L=6, XL=3, Total ~30; Database Explorer: S=9, M=11, L=5, XL=2, Total ~40; AI Storyteller: S=12, M=15, L=7, XL=5, Total ~62; AI Fortress Player: S=14, M=18, L=10, XL=2, Total ~50; Mod Manager: S=6, M=12, L=10, XL=6, Total ~44; Labor Manager: S=8, M=12, L=8, XL=3, Total ~35; CDM & Database: varies/~30; Data ETL: varies/~20. Grand total: ~260+ discrete features.

extractions.content: Canonical event type count cross-reference table: df-structures history_event_type enum = 144 (authoritative, canonical memory layout); LegendsBrowser2 events.go = 132 (production Go source); LegendsViewer-Next XMLParser.cs = 115+ (production .NET source); weblegends events/*.cpp = 94 files (production C++ source); df-narrator = generic/no per-type handling. Synthesis: df-structures defines 144 event types. LB2's 132 types represent the most complete handling implementation. The gap of 12 consists of newer event types added in DF 0.47+ that some tools have not yet implemented handlers for. Chronicler strategy: store all 144 as TEXT, implement templates for 132 LB2-handled types, graceful fallback for remaining 12.
extractions.reasoning: The Master PRD says '132 LB2-handled event types' and '144 event types' but does not contain the cross-reference table showing the source authority of each count, nor the gap analysis (144 - 132 = 12 unimplemented newer types). This is new supporting context.

extractions.content: XML Approach comparison table across reference tools: Chronicler uses lxml.etree.iterparse + root.clear() (streaming, constant memory); LegendsBrowser (Java) uses SAX XMLReader + annotation handler (streaming, constant memory); LegendsBrowser2 (Go) uses custom hand-written tokenizer NOT encoding/xml (streaming, buffered I/O); LegendsViewer-Next (.NET) uses Async XML with FilteredStream (streaming, filtered); DwarvenSurveyor (C#) uses XmlReader streaming; df-narrator (Python) uses xml.etree.ElementTree full-tree parse (full-tree, then freed). Note: LB2 chose NOT to use encoding/xml for performance reasons.
extractions.source_location: Section 6.1 Legends XML Pipeline, XML Approach table
extractions.reasoning: The Master states Chronicler uses lxml iterparse but does not include the cross-tool XML approach comparison table. The rationale for LB2's custom tokenizer choice is entirely absent.

extractions.content: Architectural Pattern: Event Rendering Pipeline from reference tools. All successful tools use: Event (typed struct) -> Context (current entity perspective) -> Template (per-type prose) -> HTML (with entity links). For Chronicler with LLM: Event (CDM row) -> Context (target entity + related entities) -> LLM prompt (with event type template) -> Narrative (with entity references marked for linking).

extractions.content: Architectural Pattern: Generic Watcher Factory Pattern from myDFHackScripts. Helper.watch() closure returns a function that compares current state to saved state. On change, logs event and updates saved state. Configurable comparison function. This is the pattern used for change detection.
extractions.reasoning: The Master REQ-ETL-013 lists the 11 change event types but does not describe the watcher factory closure pattern from myDFHackScripts that implements them.

extractions.content: Architectural Pattern: Polling + Events Hybrid (from myDFHackScripts). Use eventful subscriptions for real-time events (deaths, item creation) AND polling via dfhack.timeout for state changes (citizen count, reports, petitions). This hybrid approach catches both immediate events and gradual state transitions.
extractions.reasoning: The Master discusses eventful subscriptions and polling separately but does not articulate the design rationale for combining both approaches (real-time for discrete events vs. polling for gradual transitions).

extractions.content: Bridge Data Flow Architecture diagram: DF Game (running) -> chronicler-bridge.lua (repeat job, 100 ticks) -> JSON file on disk -> HTTP server (port 8889) -> Python watcher (chronicler watch) -> Change detector (snapshot comparison) -> PostgreSQL CDM -> Storyteller / Explorer / API.

extractions.content: DF Calendar System Python implementation: day_of_year = seconds72 // 1200 + 1; month = min((day_of_year - 1) // 28 + 1, 12); day = (day_of_year - 1) % 28 + 1. Month names: Granite, Slate, Felsite (Spring), Hematite, Malachite, Galena (Summer), Limestone, Sandstone, Timber (Autumn), Moonstone, Opal, Obsidian (Winter). Season lookup by month index.
extractions.reasoning: Master REQ-STR-024 describes the calendar formula conceptually but the SubDocument provides the complete Python implementation code with all month names enumerated and indexed. The min() guard on month calculation is new.

new_sections.title: Landscape Overview & Tool Ecosystem
new_sections.content:
The Dwarf Fortress legends/history tool ecosystem consists of six distinct categories:

| Category | Tools Analyzed | Data Source | Access Pattern |
|----------|---------------|-------------|----------------|
| **Legends Browsers** | LegendsViewer-Next, LegendsBrowser, LegendsBrowser2 | XML export files | Batch parse, in-memory, web UI |
| **Live Game Servers** | weblegends | DFHack C++ memory | Real-time HTTP, per-request render |
| **Autonomous Agents** | df-ai | DFHack C++ memory | Tick-based reactive loop |
| **Narrative Generators** | df-narrator | XML export files | Score + template, LLM-sized output |
| **Infrastructure Tools** | dfhack-client-python, DwarfFortressLogger, myDFHackScripts, DwarvenSurveyor, df-structures | Mixed (RPC, memory, Lua, XML) | Various |
| **Mod Management** | DF-Modloader, ModHearth, PyLNP, PyDwarf, Nexus Mod Manager | Filesystem + DFHack Lua | Raw parsing, profile management |

Chronicler's unique position: No existing tool combines (1) persistent database storage, (2) live fortress polling, (3) legends XML ingestion, (4) LLM-driven narrative generation, (5) worldgen monitoring, (6) mod management awareness, and (7) labor/population management in a single integrated system.
new_sections.reasoning: The Master PRD lacks a structured landscape analysis. This section provides context for why design decisions were made the way they were, and gives developers context for understanding which reference tools informed each component.

new_sections.title: Event Type Taxonomy (Canonical Reference)
new_sections.content:
### Canonical Event Type Count Cross-Reference

| Source | Event Types | Authoritative? |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| df-structures `history_event_type` enum | **144** | Yes (memory layout, canonical) |
| LegendsBrowser2 `events.go` | 132 | Yes (Go source, production) |
| LegendsViewer-Next `XMLParser.cs` | 115+ | Yes (.NET source, production) |
| weblegends `events/*.cpp` | 94 files | Yes (C++ source, production) |
| df-narrator | Generic (type string) | No |

**Synthesis**: df-structures defines 144 event types. LB2's 132 types are the most complete implementation. The 12-type gap consists of DF 0.47+ additions not yet implemented by all tools.

**Chronicler strategy**: Store all 144 types as TEXT. Implement narrative templates for 132 LB2-handled types. Graceful fallback (raw field dump + LLM) for remaining 12.

### Event Categories (Merged Taxonomy)

| Category | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|----------|
| HF Lifecycle | 15 | died, revived, wounded, abducted, enslaved, freed, ransomed |
| HF Relationships | 10 | add/remove hf_hf_link, add/remove hf_entity_link, add/remove hf_site_link |
| HF Actions | 12 | attacked_site, destroyed_site, confronted, does_interaction, preach |
| HF Intrigue | 10 | convicted, interrogated, formed_intrigue, failed_frame, sabotage |
| Artifacts | 13 | created, destroyed, lost, found, given, possessed, recovered, stored, transformed, copied, claim_formed |
| Sites/Construction | 18 | created_site, destroyed_site, site_taken_over, reclaim_site, created_structure, razed_structure |
| Entities | 14 | entity_created, dissolved, incorporated, overthrown, law, persecuted, alliance_formed |
| War/Combat | 8 | field_battle, squad_vs_squad, tactical_situation, attacked_site, plundered_site |
| Diplomacy | 10 | peace_accepted/rejected, agreement_formed/concluded/rejected, trade, first_contact |
| Culture/Art | 7 | poetic/musical/dance_form_created, written_content_composed, knowledge_discovered |
| Masterpieces | 8 | arch_construct, item, dye, item_improvement, food, engraving, lost |
| Occasions | 5 | ceremony, competition, performance, procession, gamble |
| Misc | 14 | creature_devoured, body_abused, merchant, sneak_into_site, spotted_leaving, insurrection |

### Event Collection Types (19 Types)

| Category | Types |
|----------|-------|
| Warfare | battle, war, duel, raid, site_conquered |
| Political | insurrection, persecution, purge, entity_overthrown |
| Calamities | beast_attack, abduction, theft |
| Rituals | occasion, procession, ceremony, performance, competition |
| Travel | journey |
new_sections.reasoning: The Master PRD lacks a standalone Event Type Taxonomy section. This information is critically needed for implementing REQ-STR-016 (132+ template bank), REQ-CDM-004 (144 types as TEXT), and REQ-VIS-023 (19 collection types). The consolidated taxonomy with source attribution provides implementers with authoritative reference data.

new_sections.title: Architectural Patterns & Implementation Guides
new_sections.content:
### Event Rendering Pipeline Pattern

All successful legends browsers implement this pattern:
```
Event (typed struct) -> Context (current entity perspective) -> Template (per-type prose) -> HTML (with entity links)
```

For Chronicler with LLM:
```
Event (CDM row) -> Context (target entity + related entities) -> LLM prompt (with event type template) -> Narrative (with entity references marked for linking)
```

### Polling + Events Hybrid Pattern

From myDFHackScripts: use `eventful` subscriptions for real-time discrete events (deaths, item creation) AND polling via `dfhack.timeout` for gradual state transitions (citizen count, reports, petitions). The hybrid approach ensures neither immediate events nor gradual transitions are missed.

### Generic Watcher Factory Pattern

From myDFHackScripts `Helper.watch()`: returns a closure that compares current state to saved state. On change, logs event and updates saved state. Configurable comparison function. This is the pattern used for Chronicler's 11 change event types.

### Post-Parse Processing Pattern

All reference legends browsers (LB2 `world.process()`, LV-Next post-parse) run a cross-referencing pass after XML loading. This is non-optional -- without it, entity relationships, flags, scores, and kill lists are incomplete.

### Exclusive Action Queue Pattern

From df-ai: one exclusive action chain active at a time; queue pending actions; report completion/failure before starting next. Prevents conflicting multi-step fortress management operations.
new_sections.reasoning: The Master PRD has no Architectural Patterns section. These implementation patterns are derived from cross-analysis of 17 reference repositories and provide critical design guidance for implementing the ETL pipeline, event rendering system, and AI advisor not available anywhere in the Master.

new_sections.title: Consolidated Action Items (Prioritized Implementation Backlog)
new_sections.content:
### Tier 1 -- Critical (Blocks Narrative Engine and Explorer)

| # | Action | Effort | Source |
|---|--------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Add all 144 event types to CDM taxonomy | M | df-structures |
| 2 | Extend HF CDM with missing high-priority fields (flags, interactions, skills, links, kills, whereabouts) | L | All legends browsers |
| 3 | Add importance scoring columns and compute on ingestion | S | df-narrator |
| 4 | Implement death cause narrative rendering (50+ causes) | M | weblegends |
| 5 | Implement perspective-aware event narrative generation | M | LB2, weblegends |
| 6 | Add cross-linking infrastructure (entity references become navigable links) | M | All legends browsers |
| 7 | Implement DF calendar utility (seconds72 to date/month/season) | S | df-narrator, weblegends |
| 8 | Build agentic SQL storyteller (schema summary + SQL tool) | L | Chronicler original |
| 9 | Parse remaining XML sections (world_constructions, art_forms, identities, rivers) | M | All legends browsers |
